title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the two photographs and answer the question. You have about one minute.

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what challenges journalists face in reporting the news and what qualities make good journalism.

images.label: A
images.description: A war correspondent in a flak jacket reporting live from a conflict zone, holding a microphone with smoke and ruins visible behind them

images.label: B
images.description: A journalist interviewing a politician outside a government building, holding a recorder while a camera crew films the exchange

followUp.question: What qualities do you think are most important for a journalist to have, and why?

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what the dangers of misinformation online might be and how people can protect themselves from it.

images.label: D
images.description: A screenshot of a social media feed showing a sensational headline being shared thousands of times, with no credible source listed

images.label: E
images.description: A fact-checker at a computer desk carefully analysing an article, with multiple browser tabs open comparing different sources

followUp.question: Do you think social media companies have a responsibility to remove fake news from their platforms? What are the challenges in doing this?
